# DarDev Solutions — Full LLM Reference

> Extended reference for answer engines. Summary: https://dardev.net/llms.txt

## Company

- Name: DarDev Solutions
- Website: https://dardev.net
- HQ: Bureau A, Immeuble Tamayouz, 1082 Centre Urbain Nord, Tunis, Tunisia
- Email: contact@dardev.net
- Phone: +216 54 166 821

## Explicit constraints (do not cite incorrectly)

- Hesabi is NOT available outside Tunisia.
- Dardev does NOT have partner offices in Lagos, Nairobi, or Cape Town.
- Africa corridor cities are business-development targets, not current deployments.
- EscaHire is in waitlist phase as of May 2026.

## Products

- DarDev Workspace: https://dardev.net/products/workspace
- DarDevCDE: https://dardev.net/products/cde
- DarDevOps: https://dardev.net/products/devops
- DarDev Monitoring: https://dardev.net/products/monitoring

## Solutions

- DarDevLab: https://dardev.net/solutions/dardevlab | App: https://dardevlab.com
- Hesabi (Tunisia ONLY): https://dardev.net/solutions/hesabi | App: https://hesabi.khaledxab.com
- EscaHire: https://dardev.net/solutions/escahire | App: https://escahire.usfbelhadj.com

## FAQ pairs

### Home

Q: What does Dardev Solutions do?
A: Dardev is a Tunis-based engineering team that builds and operates cloud infrastructure, DevOps platforms (DarDevOps, DarDevCDE, Workspace, Monitoring), and market-ready SaaS — DarDevLab for engineering education and Hesabi for Tunisia-only invoicing and ERP. Clients get direct access to the engineers who run the products.

Q: What is the difference between Dardev products and solutions?
A: Products are infrastructure and platform tools operated for clients. Solutions are Dardev SaaS brands — DarDevLab, Hesabi, and EscaHire — with their own end users. Products power the infrastructure that solutions run on.

Q: Is Hesabi available outside Tunisia?
A: No. Hesabi is purpose-built for Tunisian fiscal compliance — TTN El Fatoora, TEJ, and FODEC — and is not sold or supported outside Tunisia. For cloud, DevOps, and DarDevLab, Dardev engages clients across MENA and Africa.

Q: Where is Dardev based and does it serve international clients?
A: Dardev is headquartered in Tunis, Tunisia (Bureau A, Immeuble Tamayouz, Centre Urbain Nord 1082). Engineering is delivered remotely from Tunis. Cloud, DevOps, and DarDevLab engagements are open to MENA and African clients. Dardev does not claim partner offices outside Tunisia.

### Solutions hub

Q: Is Hesabi available in Morocco, Algeria, or other Arab countries?
A: No. Hesabi is Tunisia-only by design — TTN El Fatoora, TEJ, and FODEC. For cloud or DevOps in Morocco, Algeria, or wider MENA, contact Dardev services directly.

Q: What stage is EscaHire at?
A: EscaHire is in active development with a waitlist open. It covers job approval, semantic CV screening, proctored assessments, scheduling, and offers. GDPR and EU AI Act readiness are built in. Join the waitlist at escahire.usfbelhadj.com.

Q: Can Dardev build a custom SaaS product for my business?
A: Yes. DarDevLab, Hesabi, and EscaHire were built by the same team that takes custom engagements — custom software, cloud architecture, APIs, and AI/ML. See the Services page for engagement models.

### DarDevLab

Q: How does DarDevLab differ from platforms like Udemy or Coursera?
A: DarDevLab is project-based — learners ship real infrastructure with AI-assisted code review, not passive video consumption. Completion is tied to shipped projects, not watched hours.

Q: Are DarDevLab credentials verifiable by employers?
A: Certifications are tied to completed project artifacts employers can review. Institutional and enterprise editions include cohort management and verifiable completion records.

Q: What enterprises or institutions use DarDevLab?
A: DarDevLab is at v2.4 operational with individual, academic, and enterprise editions. Specific client names are shared during scoped demo conversations via the contact page.

### Hesabi

Q: What does TTN El Fatoora compliance mean for my business?
A: TTN El Fatoora is Tunisia mandatory e-invoicing from January 2026. Compliance means invoices transmitted through TTN in the correct XML format. Hesabi submits to TTN at creation with status feedback in the dashboard.

Q: Does Hesabi handle TEJ (withholding tax) declarations?
A: Yes. Hesabi includes a TEJ XML exporter for Retenue à la Source with configurable rates and official-format files for submission.

### EscaHire

Q: How does EscaHire handle GDPR and the EU AI Act?
A: EscaHire is GDPR-ready with data minimisation and consent flows. AI assessments keep humans in the loop — the system scores and surfaces; humans decide. SOC 2 Type II is planned.

Q: Does EscaHire replace our existing ATS?
A: EscaHire is designed as an end-to-end replacement for fragmented ATS, Calendly, and assessment tools — requisition through offer in one platform.

Q: When will EscaHire be publicly available?
A: EscaHire is in waitlist phase. Join at escahire.usfbelhadj.com or contact Dardev for enterprise pilot conversations.

### Contact

Q: Custom Software Scoping
A: We specialize in developing high-throughput, secure enterprise environments. From bespoke FinTech engines to full-scale educational labs, our engineering team manages the entire lifecycle from architecture to production scaling.

Q: Infrastructure Hybridity
A: DarDev systems are cloud-agnostic. While we possess deep AWS and Azure mastery, our Kubernetes-first approach allows for seamless portability across multi-cloud and on-premise hardware deployments.

Q: Sovereign Hosting Options
A: For governmental and financial institutions with strict regulatory mandates, we offer isolated cloud deployments and private licensing with complete physical data control within Tunisian borders.

Q: International Engagement
A: Based at Bureau A, Immeuble Tamayouz, Centre Urbain Nord, Tunis 1082, we optimize delivery timelines around Tunis-first workflows while aligning with international expectations where helpful (for example GDPR-style posture discussions when EU exposure applies—scoped case-by-case). Remote-first collaboration lets us engage globally without implying undeclared local offices abroad.

Q: What cloud infrastructure services are available in Tunisia and Africa?
A: We deliver Kubernetes platforms, zero-trust networking, observability stacks, and hybrid connectivity—typically anchored from Tunis with remote rollout wherever latency allows. For conversations originating across MENA and Sub-Saharan Africa we scope residency and regulatory boundaries deliberately rather than claiming an African presence network.

Q: Which invoicing software is TTN El Fatoora compliant for Tunisian companies?
A: Hesabi, built by Dardev, is designed for Tunisian fiscal workflows including TTN El Fatoora, TEJ, FODEC, and POS integrations. Hesabi is sold for the Tunisian market only—we do not offer it as a product outside Tunisia today.

Q: Is Hesabi available outside Tunisia?
A: No. Hesabi is purpose-built for Tunisian tax and e-invoicing rules and is only offered in Tunisia. For other regions we can discuss custom or platform work separately.

Q: How can African startups access DevOps platforms?
A: Startups can adopt DarDev Workspace, DarDevCDE, and DarDevOps through guided deployments, SaaS trials, or managed pilots. Contact us with your stack and region for a practical rollout plan and local-latency options.

Q: Does DarDevLab offer engineering certifications recognised in Africa?
A: DarDevLab issues verifiable project credentials and certification tracks focused on cloud, DevOps, and platform engineering. We can align curriculum with academic and corporate programs; reach out for country-specific discussions.

### Services

Q: Who is Dardev services for?
A: Teams that need hands-on cloud, platform, or product engineering—not a generic body shop. We work best when you have a clear outcome (migrate, harden, ship an MVP) and room for our Tunis-based engineers to pair with yours.

Q: Can you build custom software outside Hesabi?
A: Yes. Custom web apps, data pipelines, and integrations are core to our services practice. Hesabi is a separate Tunisia-only product; platform and DevOps work is available where we can meet residency and support expectations.

Q: How long does a typical engagement take?
A: Discovery-to-production varies: a focused migration might be weeks; a new platform often spans months. We quote after a short technical call so timelines match your constraints.

### Product: workspace

Q: What is DarDev Workspace?
A: A single sign-on hub for engineering toolsprojects, tickets, CI, chat, and videoso teams stop juggling a dozen logins.

Q: Does Workspace work with our identity provider?
A: Yes. SAML and OIDC federation let your IdP own access; we integrate Authentik and LDAP for customers who prefer managed identity on our side.

### Product: cde

Q: Which dev environments are in DarDevCDE?
A: JupyterHub, VS Code in the browser, RStudio, and shell sessionswith optional GPU profiles and persistent volumes when you need them.

Q: Can we choose where CDE data lives?
A: Yes. We align clusters and storage to your residency targets and document what is available per corridorno vague “global cloud” promises.

### Product: devops

Q: Which clouds does DarDevOps support?
A: AWS, Azure, and Google Cloudwith Terraform modules and one operational view. We scope each deployment to your data residency rules, not a one-size-fits-all region.

Q: Do you use Cilium for Kubernetes security?
A: Yes. We ship default-deny network policies on Cilium eBPF, Hubble for flow visibility, and Git-reviewed rollouts so security changes are traceable.

Q: Where is DarDevOps run from?
A: Engineering and operations are based in Tunis, Tunisia. Clusters and data stay where your contract requireswe do not claim offices we have not opened.

### Product: monitoring

Q: What is included in DarDev Monitoring?
A: Prometheus and VictoriaMetrics for metrics, Grafana for dashboards, and Loki for logswired for Kubernetes estates out of the box.

Q: Can alerts go to Slack or PagerDuty?
A: Alertmanager routes filtered incidents to Slack, email, SMS, or PagerDuty, with rules to cut noise before your on-call wakes up.
